titlestmt.titleproper: Miriam Seidman Blaukopf nee Blasenstein collection6916
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-08-10 18:42:57 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Miriam Seidman Blaukopf nee Blasenstein collection
did.abstract: Correspondence, photographs, tape recordings and other material regarding Polish holocaust survivors.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Miriam Seidman Blaukopf survived four years in hiding from the Nazis in German-occupied Poland. Her granddaughter, Nina Peterson, interviewed her and compiled other information about her.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Reparations and other personal correspondence, Leo and Miriam Blaukopf, 1957-1968. Original correspondence, notes, and translations, including letters from family and friends, 1937-62, letters from Isaac Miller to Miriam, 1914-1930; notes and maps by Nina Peterson; letters from Miriam to America, 1945-46 (3 letters translated by Nina Peterson); translations of selected notes and communications by Murray Sachs and Nina Peterson. Passports, identification paper, and immigration papers for Leo Blaukopf, Miriam Blaukopf, and Nina Peterson; newspaper articles from German and Austrian papers (and "California Farmer" article on Adolph Miller, 1954). Three unpublished manuscripts written by Miriam Seidman Blaukopf include biography beginning with Krystallnacht in 1938 when she was still in Vienna, continuing with her return to Poland and ending with her liberation and trek to relocation authorities in Rome in 1945; earlier years - childhood and marriage; and brief summary manuscript. Manuscript "Ferramonti di Tarsi," by Nina Wekeler, typewritten in German, 1941, about the internment camp in Italy where Leo Blaukopf was sent. Also, photographs; tape recordings of interviews with Miriam by Nina Peterson, with CD copies.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Copyright retained by donor and her heirs and assigns.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Miriam Seidman Blaukopf nee Blasenstein collection, #6916. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Biographical information, family tree, timeline, reel-to-reel tape/CD guide
Leo and Miriam Blaukopf correspondence and reparations
Leo and Miriam Blaukopf correspondence and reparations
Miriam Blaukopf pensions
Miriam Blaukopf correspondence
Kurt and Hilde Blaukopf correspondence
Correspondence from friends and family
Correspondence from Isaac(IJ) Miller to Miriam Blaukopf

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1 photograph, one translation by Murray Sachs

did.unittitle: Necha (Nina) Peterson, notes and maps
Correspondence from Miriam Blaukopf to America

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes translations of three letters by Necha(Nina) Peterson

did.unittitle: Correspondence, notes and translations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translations of selected notes and correspondence by Murray Sachs and Necha(Nina) Peterson

did.unittitle: Leo Blaukopf passports, identification transit papers, birth record
Newspaper articles, booklet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes picture of Buczacz, Poland community members, Einstein Memorial article, booklet with photographs of Bergen-Belsen camp and memorial for unknown Jewish martyrs, articles from Zeitspiegel Austrian Weekly, Jung Osterreich, Aufbau and California Farmer

did.unittitle: Immigration papers, marriage certificate

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of passport for Necha(Nina) Peterson, marriage certificate and naturalization papers for Leo Blaukopf and Miriam Seitman

did.unittitle: Manuscripts, handwritten in Yiddish by Miriam Blaukopf

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript A - begins with Krystallnacht in 1938 when Miriam Blaukopf was still living in Vienna. It continues with her return to Poland and ends with her liberation and trek through several countries to relocation authorities in Rome in 1945. Manuscript B - childhood and marriage. Manuscript C - brief summary of the first two manuscripts.

did.unittitle: Ferramonti[di Tarsi] by Nina Wekeler
Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some originals, some photocopies. Includes master list and approximate dates.

did.unittitle: Reel-to-reel tape - Necha(Nina) Peterson interviews with Mariam Blaukopf in Yiddish. Interview 1 - childhood Kolanki, Interview 12 - Bindergasse, 18-20 years.
Reel-to-reel tape - Necha(Nina) Peterson interviews with Mariam Blaukopf in Yiddish. Interview 2 - Horodenka, marriage, family, Kotikovka, pre-WWI. Interview 11 - Tlusty, Bindergasse, Horodenka.

Reel-to-reel tape - Necha(Nina) Peterson interviews with Mariam Blaukopf in Yiddish. Interview 3 - WWI, Vienna, school. Interview 10 - German translation, Avrom hospital, Beile, Horodenka, Kommandante, 1939 - 1940, Gestapo, December 4, 1940.

Reel-to-reel tape - Necha(Nina) Peterson interviews with Mariam Blaukopf in Yiddish. Interview 4 - Vienna, Anschloss, 18 years Wohlmutstrasse, 28 years Adoplh comes to Horodenka. Interview 9 - German translation, Horodenka, Beile.

Reel-to-reel tape - Necha(Nina) Peterson interviews with Mariam Blaukopf in Yiddish. Interview 5 - Return to Poland from Vienna, Berlin, Beuten, Katowicz, Tandelmarktgasse, Austria, Horodenka, November 1938. Interview 8 - German Translation, Nazi invasion of Horodenka 1939.
Reel-to-reel tape - Necha(Nina) Peterson interviews with Mariam Blaukopf in Yiddish. Interview 6 - Vienna 1938, Bindergasse, Horodenka. Interview 7 - Vienna, German translation.
Reel-to-reel tape - Necha(Nina) Peterson interviews with Mariam Blaukopf in Yiddish. Interview 13 - Father in hospital, Horodenka, Kolanki, Dr. Karpf. Interview 14 - Kolanki, end of story, America.

Reel-to-reel tape - Necha(Nina) Peterson interviews with Mariam Blaukopf in Yiddish. Interview 15 - Tluste, Czortkov, back to Horodenka, Dniester river, Feige's children, Bucharest. Interview 16 - Horodenka, Chernovitz, Bucharest,Hungary, Mamarocz-Szigeth, Graz.
CD of tape interviews 1,2,3,4,5.
CD of tape interviews 6, 13 15.
CD of tape interviews 7,8,9,10,11.
CD of tape interviews 12, 14,16.
CD of three tape interviews - 4,6,12. Filtered by a recording technician.
